name: OmopPatientPersonView
title: Patient → person (FHIR-flat, stage 1)
description:
Stage 1 of FHIR→OMOP: produce one flat row per Patient, preserving raw FHIR values. Stage 2 SQL does ConceptMap lookups (gender / race / ethnicity), resolves location FK, and assigns surrogate person_id.

Uses US Core extensions for race / ethnicity / birthsex. Birthsex preferred over Patient.gender for gender_concept_id (closer to OMOP semantics for biological sex).

column.name: id
column.description: FHIR Patient.id (Synthea UUID). → person_source_value + surrogate person_id.

column.name: gender
column.description: male/female/other/unknown. Fallback when birthsex absent. → gender_source_value, optional gender_concept_id.

column.name: us_core_birthsex
column.description: Biological sex at birth (M/F). Preferred for gender_concept_id over Patient.gender.

column.name: birth_date
column.description: → year/month/day_of_birth (via EXTRACT) and birth_datetime (cast).

column.name: birth_time
column.description: Optional FHIR patient-birthTime extension on the birthDate element. When present, used to populate birth_datetime with the actual time-of-day; otherwise birth_datetime falls back to birthDate at midnight. Synthea does not emit this extension.

column.name: omop_race_code
column.description: OMOP-native race: Athena Race concept_id as string. When present, copied directly into person.race_concept_id (no ConceptMap lookup). Preferred over US Core race.

column.name: omop_race_display
column.description: Human-readable label from the omop-race extension. → race_source_value (only when omop_race_code is set).

column.name: race_omb_code
column.description: OMB race ombCategory code (2106-3/2054-5/…/UNK). Fallback when omop_race_code absent: resolved via cm.race_omb_to_omop.

column.name: race_text
column.description: Human-readable race label from US Core. → race_source_value when omop_race_display absent.

column.name: omop_ethnicity_code
column.description: OMOP-native ethnicity: Athena Ethnicity concept_id (38003563 / 38003564) as string. When present, copied directly into person.ethnicity_concept_id. Preferred over US Core ethnicity.

column.name: omop_ethnicity_display
column.description: Human-readable label from the omop-ethnicity extension. → ethnicity_source_value (only when omop_ethnicity_code is set).

column.name: ethnicity_omb_code
column.description: OMB ethnicity ombCategory code (2135-2 Hispanic / 2186-5 Not Hispanic). Fallback when omop_ethnicity_code absent: resolved via cm.ethnicity_omb_to_omop.

column.name: ethnicity_text
column.description: Human-readable ethnicity label from US Core. → ethnicity_source_value when omop_ethnicity_display absent.

column.name: general_practitioner_ref
column.description: Reference string ('Practitioner/<uuid>' or 'urn:uuid:<uuid>'). → provider_id via tailing segment lookup.

column.name: managing_organization_ref
column.description: → care_site_id via tailing segment.

column.name: location_line
column.description: → cdm.location.address_1. Joins multi-line addresses with space. Prefers use='official' address, falls back to first.

column.name: location_city
column.description: → cdm.location.city. Used in composite key when matching existing location.

column.name: location_state
column.description: → cdm.location.state. Synthea writes 2-letter codes (e.g. 'MA').

column.name: location_zip
column.description: → cdm.location.zip. 5-digit US ZIP (FHIR side preserves leading zero, unlike ETL-Synthea's R fread).

column.name: location_county
column.description: → cdm.location.county. Mapped from FHIR Address.district. Synthea omits this in its FHIR export (only present in CSV).

column.name: location_country
column.description: → cdm.location.country_source_value + lookup → country_concept_id via cm.country_iso_to_omop.

column.name: location_lat
column.description: → cdm.location.latitude. From FHIR geolocation extension on Address.

column.name: location_lng
column.description: → cdm.location.longitude.
